_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-26R Hydroxocobalamin (MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-26R: Hydroxocobalamin — MDHHS Reviewed 2025
pharmacological_category: Antidote — Cyanide Poisoning / Vitamin B12 Analog
indications: Smoke inhalation with altered mental status or severe respiratory distress (structural fire victims — cyanide suspected)
contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to hydroxocobalamin or cyanocobalamin
No absolute contraindications in life-threatening cyanide poisoning
precautions: Incompatible with dopamine and fentanyl — do NOT mix or give through same IV line as these medications; flush line before/after
Will cause skin, urine, and mucous membrane discoloration (red-brown / burgundy) — warn patient and document; not a complication
Reconstitution required: follow Cyanokit instructions using 0.9% NaCl only
Reconstitution technique: invert and rock gently for specified time — do NOT shake vigorously
expected_effects: Binds cyanide ions to form cyanocobalamin (non-toxic) — directly detoxifies cyanide
Improvement in hemodynamic status, mental status, and respiratory function in cyanide poisoning
side_effects: Skin/urine/mucous membrane reddish-brown discoloration (expected — harmless and transient)
notes: Available as Cyanokit — 5 gram vial or 2.5 gram vial
Reconstitution: add 0.9% NaCl (200 mL for 5 g kit; 100 mL for 2.5 g kit)
Mix: invert and rock — 5 g: 60 seconds; 2.5 g: 30 seconds
Solution is dark red — this is normal
Incompatible with dopamine and fentanyl — flush IV line before and after
Discoloration of urine/skin is expected and harmless; may interfere with co-oximetry readings

dosing.population: Adult (>14 yr) — cyanide poisoning / smoke inhalation with AMS or severe respiratory distress
dosing.dose: 5 grams (full Cyanokit) OR 2.5 grams infusion over 15 minutes
dosing.notes: Reconstitute with 0.9% NaCl only; invert/rock per kit instructions; infuse over 15 min; incompatible with dopamine and fentanyl
